```python import smtplib from email.mime.text import MIMEText from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart class EmailService: def __init__(self, smtp_server, smtp_port, smtp_username, smtp_password): self.smtp_server = smtp_server self.smtp_port = smtp_port self.smtp_username = smtp_username self.smtp_password = smtp_password self.server = None # Initialize server to None def connect(self): """Connect to the SMTP server.""" try: self.server = smtplib.SMTP(self.smtp_server, self.smtp_port) self.server.starttls() self.server.login(self.smtp_username, self.smtp_password) print("Successfully connected to the SMTP server.") return True except Exception as e: print(f"Failed to connect to the SMTP server: {e}") return False def disconnect(self): """Disconnect from the SMTP server.""" if self.server: try: self.server.quit() print("Successfully disconnected from the SMTP server.") except Exception as e: print(f"Failed to disconnect gracefully: {e}") finally: self.server = None def send_email(self, sender_email, recipient_email, subject, body, is_html=False): """Sends an email.""" if not self.server: print("Not connected to SMTP server. Call connect() first.") return False message = MIMEMultipart("alternative") message["Subject"] = subject message["From"] = sender_email message["To"] = recipient_email if is_html: part = MIMEText(body, "html") else: part = MIMEText(body, "plain") message.attach(part) try: self.server.sendmail(sender_email, recipient_email, message.as_string()) print(f"Email sent successfully to {recipient_email}") return True except Exception as e: print(f"Failed to send email: {e}") return False def send_invitation(self, sender_email, recipient_email, interview_time, interview_link): """Sends an interview invitation email.""" subject = "Interview Invitation" body = f""" Dear Candidate, You are invited to an interview on {interview_time}. Please join the interview using the following link: {interview_link} Best regards, The Hiring Team """ return self.send_email(sender_email, recipient_email, subject, body) def send_reminder(self, sender_email, recipient_email, interview_time, interview_link): """Sends an interview reminder email.""" subject = "Interview Reminder" body = f""" Dear Candidate, This is a reminder for your interview scheduled on {interview_time}. Please join the interview using the following link: {interview_link} Best regards, The Hiring Team """ return self.send_email(sender_email, recipient_email, subject, body) def send_notification(self, sender_email, recipient_email, message_body): """Sends a general notification email.""" subject = "Notification" return self.send_email(sender_email, recipient_email, subject, message_body) if __name__ == '__main__': # Example Usage (replace with your actual credentials) smtp_server = "your_smtp_server" # e.g., "smtp.gmail.com" smtp_port = 587 # Or 465 for SSL smtp_username = "your_email@example.com" smtp_password = "your_password" # Consider using environment variables for security email_service = EmailService(smtp_server, smtp_port, smtp_username, smtp_password) # Connect to the SMTP server if email_service.connect(): sender_email = "your_email@example.com" recipient_email = "candidate@example.com" # Send Invitation interview_time = "2024-01-20 10:00 AM" interview_link = "https://meet.google.com/xyz" email_service.send_invitation(sender_email, recipient_email, interview_time, interview_link) # Send Reminder email_service.send_reminder(sender_email, recipient_email, interview_time, interview_link) # Send Notification notification_message = "Your application has been received." email_service.send_notification(sender_email, recipient_email, notification_message) # Send HTML email html_body = """

Dear Candidate,

Thank you for your interest in our company!

We are excited about your application.

""" email_service.send_email(sender_email, recipient_email, "Application Received", html_body, is_html=True) # Disconnect from the SMTP server email_service.disconnect() ```
